title: Crypto Brought Trump a Huge Windfall, Even as Many Investors Lost Big
summary: President Trump and his family reaped vast financial rewards from a memecoin that generated losses for hundreds of thousands of investors.

title: Does Trump Worry About Conflicts of Interest? ‘I Found Out That Nobody Cared.’
summary: American presidents have generally tried to avoid appearing to profit from the office. President Trump has chosen a different path.

summary: President Trump’s business holdings, which garnered him more than $2 billion last year, create potential conflicts of interest surpassing any predecessor.

title: Promising Much to Many, Johnson Loses His Grip on the House
summary: Republicans are growing frustrated with Mr. Johnson’s approach to governing with a razor-thin majority, saying that he promises more than he can achieve, frustrating the disparate groups in his caucus.

title: Colorado Governor Fires Officials Who Opposed Freeing Tina Peters
summary: Gov. Jared Polis commuted the sentence of Tina Peters, who tampered with voting machines in an attempt to show that the 2020 election had been rigged against President Trump.

title: The Democratic Incumbents Most at Risk of Losing to Progressive Primary Challengers in 2026
summary: A wave of outsider fervor has already knocked out a number of incumbents in primary races. Several more face spirited opposition.

title: Judge Blocks Postal Service From Imposing Restrictions on Mail-In Ballots
summary: The ruling, based on agreements the service made in a 2021 legal settlement, was the second time a judge has blocked new curbs by President Trump on voting by mail.

title: Trump Remakes Washington, D.C., Into a Maze of Fences and Guard Members
summary: President Trump seems to have turned swaths of the city into either a construction zone or an armed camp as he seeks to prove that he alone can improve it.

title: America, Onscreen
summary: Ten writers on the films that get at the hopes, dreams, heartbreak and hilarity of the U.S.A. as it turns 250.

title: What America’s 250th Means to Mamdani
summary: Mayor Zohran Mamdani has not actively pushed New York City’s role in the nation’s anniversary celebrations, in part because of the complexities he sees in the country’s past and present.

title: Trump Finds His Presidential Alter Ego, the ‘He-Man’ Teddy Roosevelt
summary: President Trump flew into town on the new Air Force One and spent time touring the library dedicated to Theodore Roosevelt, whom he called “a great he-man.”

title: Immigrant Arrests Surge to 10,000 in 5 Days as ICE Clamps Down
summary: The agency has doubled its daily arrest numbers without the fanfare of last year’s large urban operations, sowing fear in immigrant communities.

title: Trump Administration Delivers Lucrative Win for Its Kratom Allies
summary: In moving to ban a potent synthetic version of kratom, the president’s team paved the way for more sales for makers of rival botanic supplements, who had aggressively lobbied for the change.

title: Bureau of Prisons Will Close Facilities Housing Thousands of Inmates
summary: The agency pointed to crumbling infrastructure, chronic staffing shortages and budget shortfalls as it moved to cut costs.

summary: The Trump administration had halted the shipments to Iraq as part of its efforts to pressure the Baghdad government to distance itself from Iran.

summary: The vice president, a staunch critic of foreign wars, accused the president’s detractors of trying to prolong a conflict the administration was trying to end.

summary: While Republicans celebrated the ruling, many Democrats stayed quiet on an issue that had proved divisive in the last election.

summary: The German software giant SAP says it is betting that employees can reinvent jobs instead of eliminating them. Experts are divided on whether it will work.

summary: On social media, he said the Village People’s lead singer, Victor Willis, was with him “right from the beginning.” But the president has a more complicated history with the group.

title: Victor Willis, Lead Singer of the Village People, Dies at 74
summary: Often performing dressed as a helmeted police officer, he also co-wrote the group’s biggest hits, including “Y.M.C.A.” and “Macho Man.”

title: Climbers Get Engaged Atop Empire State Building. Then They Get Arrested.
summary: After two extreme climbers ascended the landmark’s 200-foot needle, one proposed to the other, before they were apprehended by the police.

title: The World Has Failed the Uyghurs
summary: If the global community can’t stop China’s atrocities against the Uyghurs, it must help us keep our culture alive in exile.

title: U.S.A.I.D. Cuts Killed People. That’s the Truth.
